page: Amazon Web Services c8id.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server instance powered by Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors. The hardware configuration features 192 virtual CPUs across 96 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. It includes a massive 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix operations. The instance is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, yielding a compute-focused ratio of 2.0 GB per core. Local storage is highly substantial, consisting of 11400 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes, complemented by a baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps.

Benchmark data highlights the top-tier multi-core capabilities of this bare-metal instance. In multi-threaded processing, the server achieves top-tier performance, as evidenced by a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 209,156.22 and a bzip3 multi-threaded decompression rate of 1281.41 GB/sec. Memory bandwidth is exceptionally high, reaching 15499.59 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 599.05 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. The presence of AMX instructions enables strong performance in CPU-based machine learning workloads, processing 337.95 tokens per second for medium 7B LLM prompts. However, single-core performance represents a relative tradeoff, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance placing in the average tier.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c8id.metal-48xl offers high resource density by combining massive compute power with a very large local NVMe storage footprint on a single bare-metal node. This eliminates hypervisor overhead and makes it highly efficient for workloads requiring direct hardware access and high-speed local I/O. The server is optimally suited for high-performance computing, large-scale database operations, software compilation, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services c8id.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server featuring 192 vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors running at 3.9 GHz. It is configured with 384.0 GB of system memory and a massive 11400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, alongside a 75 Gbps baseline network speed. The architecture supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX, enabling top-tier multi-core performance, high memory bandwidth, and rapid database operations. While single-core performance sits in the average tier, its multi-threaded capabilities and high-speed local storage make it highly efficient for demanding workloads. This bare-metal instance is designed for high-performance computing, intensive database operations, software compilation, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8id.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server with 192 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 11400 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for intensive database and HPC workloads.

tagline: A bare-metal, compute-optimized platform featuring 192 vCPUs, massive local NVMe storage, and advanced matrix acceleration for high-throughput enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 192 vCPUs and 3.9 GHz clock speed
11400 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD instance storage
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated matrix and vector operations
75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput data transfer
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance
Best suited for high-performance computing, large databases, and CPU-based LLM inference
